expected: Represent chronic liver injury context as relevant background.
Do not imply AFP is universally elevated.
Treatment discussion should acknowledge stage or context dependence.
forbidden: Claim that AFP alone diagnoses every case.
Present one universal therapy as appropriate for all patients.
graderNotes: Assess factual caution, not maximal detail.

expected: Explain insulin deficiency with ketosis and acidosis.
Include fluid/electrolyte management logic.
Differentiate treatment from simple glucose lowering alone.
forbidden: Treat DKA as hyperglycemia only.
Ignore potassium considerations entirely.
graderNotes: Allow phrasing variation; preserve core pathophysiology.

expected: Include infectious/alveolar process.
Symptoms should plausibly include respiratory complaints.
Management should link antimicrobial treatment to the cause while acknowledging severity context.
forbidden: Describe pneumonia as purely airway spasm.
Ignore diagnostic imaging/context entirely.
graderNotes: Atypical presentations allowed if labeled as such.

expected: Weakness pattern should be consistent with neuromuscular junction disease.
The villain metaphor should map to impaired transmission rather than muscle destruction.
Treatment logic should reflect mechanism.
forbidden: Confuse MG with primary demyelinating neuropathy.
Treat fixed sensory loss as the core feature.

expected: Airflow obstruction and bronchospasm remain central.
Treatment logic reflects bronchodilation and inflammation control.
Do not confuse asthma with alveolar consolidation.
forbidden: Present asthma as primarily alveolar infection.
Ignore airway narrowing and wheeze logic.
graderNotes: Respiratory crisis content should keep airway mechanics clear.

expected: Congestion and impaired forward flow are treated as linked problems.
Symptoms reflect volume overload or perfusion compromise.
Management reflects stabilization plus cause-aware therapy.
forbidden: Reduce the condition to isolated hypertension.
Ignore pulmonary edema or volume logic entirely.

expected: Focal neurologic deficit logic is preserved.
Rapid reperfusion framing reflects time-sensitive care.
Avoid collapsing ischemic and hemorrhagic mechanisms into one.
forbidden: Describe all stroke care as delayed elective management.
Confuse ischemic and hemorrhagic mechanisms with no distinction.
graderNotes: Time sensitivity is part of the core accuracy bar.

expected: Meningeal inflammation and infection remain central.
Urgency of antibiotics and supportive care is preserved.
Do not flatten this into a mild viral syndrome.
forbidden: Treat bacterial meningitis as routine self-limited illness.
Ignore meningismus, altered mentation, or sepsis risk entirely.
graderNotes: High-risk infections need urgency without sensationalism.

expected: Colonic mucosal inflammation remains central.
Bloody diarrhea and flare severity are represented fairly.
Treatment logic reflects anti-inflammatory escalation rather than one universal fix.
forbidden: Describe ulcerative colitis flare as small-bowel disease only.
Ignore severity context and flare management.
graderNotes: Inflammatory bowel disease content should stay anatomically grounded.

expected: Microvascular obstruction and pain crisis remain central.
Supportive care and trigger assessment are represented.
Do not imply infection or iron overload is the core mechanism of every crisis.
forbidden: Treat vaso-occlusive crisis as simple dehydration only.
Ignore pain-control and complication monitoring.
graderNotes: Crisis mechanism should remain specific and humane.
